1\. Project Name: Predicting Passenger Survival on the Titanic using Data Science

2\. Project Description: This project applies data science techniques to analyze the Titanic dataset and predict passenger survival. It includes data cleaning, exploratory data analysis (EDA), feature engineering, and model building using machine learning algorithms to uncover key factors influencing survival.

3\. Project Problem Statement: Prepare a model using logical regression to check whether a passenger survived or not from a titanic dataset

4\. Project Dataset Description: The dataset used in this project is the Titanic Dataset, which contains information about passengers aboard the RMS Titanic. The goal of this dataset is to analyze passenger data and predict whether a passenger survived or not.

5\. Technologies Used:

a. Programming Language: Python V 3.14.3

b. Library and Frameworks:

i. Pandas: It is used for data manipulation and data preprocessing.

ii. NumPy: It is used for numerical operations and array handling.

iii. Matplotlib: It is used for creating basic data visualizations.

iv. Seaborn: It is used for advanced and statistical data visualization.

v. Scikit- Learn: It is used for data splitting, Model Building, Model evaluation.

c. Machine Learning Algorithm: In this model, I used linear regression which classifies survived or not survived from titanic dataset.

d. Development Tools: I used Jupiter notebook for writing and executing code.

e. Git and GitHub: I used Git and GitHub used for version control and project sharing.

f. Name of the Dataset and its source: I used Titanic Dataset and I downloaded from a dataset website i.e. Kaggle.
